title: Review process and supervision design
description: Read Elixir code and report unsupervised processes, blocking GenServer loops, and faked shared state.
body: Use when asked whether Elixir concurrency code is sound. Actions: inspect spawned/started processes for supervision, check each GenServer handle_call/handle_cast for slow I/O or CPU work that blocks the serial loop, and look for shared state faked via the process dictionary, module attributes, or globals instead of a process/ETS. Do not assume a spawned process is supervised or that a cast avoids a backup. Report and record each unsupervised stateful process, blocking handler, and faked-global with file and line. This skill is read-only and must not restructure the supervision tree without runtime permission. Validation signals include confirming a killed process restarts to a clean state. Risk boundary: flag an unsupervised stateful process as a no-recovery defect and a blocking handle_call as a system-wide bottleneck.
